title: Vibe Coding Is Not Lazy Coding (Using Windsurf)
author: Prashanth Yejje; Prasanth Yejje
text:
Vibe Coding Is Not Lazy Coding (Using Windsurf)
Coding used to feel heavy.
Too many files. Too much typing. Too many tiny mistakes.
Then I tried vibe coding with Windsurf.
And no, vibe coding is not “AI doing everything.”
It’s more like… you focus on what to build, not how every line looks.
What Vibe Coding Really Means
Vibe coding means you explain your idea in plain words, and the tool helps turn it into code.
With Windsurf, you stay in flow.
You don’t stop to Google syntax or fix small errors again and again.
You think about the feature.
The tool helps with the rest.
That’s the vibe.
Why Vibe Coding Is Important
Most of our time is not spent on big ideas.
It’s spent on boring stuff:
- writing the same code again
- fixing small bugs
- jumping between files
- losing focus
Vibe coding removes this pain.
It lets your brain focus on logic and ideas.
How It Improves Productivity
Vibe coding doesn’t just make you faster.
It makes you calmer.
With Windsurf, you can:
- try ideas quickly
- change things without stress
- build first versions fast
- stay focused longer
You spend less time fighting code and more time shaping it.
That’s a big win.
What You Should Be Careful About
Here’s the real warning.
AI can give code that works but you don’t fully understand.
That’s risky.
If you blindly accept everything:
- bugs hide easily
- systems become weak
- your own skills slowly fade
Simple rule:
Let AI write code.
You decide if it makes sense.
Are We All Moving to Vibe Coding?
Yes. This future is locked in.
But one thing matters a lot:
You should learn coding before full vibe coding.
Why?
Because experience gives you judgment.
If you know the basics, you can:
- spot bad solutions
- guide AI better
- ask smarter questions
Without that, you’re not coding.
You’re just trusting luck.
Final Thought
Windsurf doesn’t replace developers.
It changes how we work.
Less typing.
More thinking.
More creativity.
Vibe coding is not about doing less work.
It’s about doing the right work.
And honestly?
That feels like the future.
